name: Whispering
tagline: A component within a larger open-source project, not a standalone tool.
description: Whispering appears to be a subdirectory or app within the Epicenter monorepo, an open-source, local-first apps collection. It is not a standalone tool with its own identity, documentation, or community.
concerns: No standalone GitHub repository or stars/forks data available.
The website is a GitHub directory within a larger project, not a dedicated tool page.
No evidence of independent functionality, documentation, or community.
Cannot verify features, pricing, or active maintenance.
fullReport: The tool 'Whispering' was discovered via Hacker News Show HN. The provided website URL points to a subdirectory within the Epicenter monorepo on GitHub. The website is reachable but is not a dedicated tool page; it is a folder containing code for an app called 'whispering' within a larger project. There is no standalone GitHub repository for Whispering, so no stars, forks, or community metrics are available. The description from the website meta is generic to the Epicenter project. Without a dedicated repository, documentation, or any evidence of independent existence, Whispering cannot be verified as a standalone software tool. It is likely a component or example app within the Epicenter ecosystem. Therefore, the tool is rejected due to insufficient evidence and lack of standalone identity.
